signals.headline: **Fed rate decision (Dec 2025)**: Powell described the economy as "very unusual" with tariff-driven goods inflation peak
signals.raw: **Fed rate decision (Dec 2025)**: Powell described the economy as "very unusual" with tariff-driven goods inflation peaking Q1 2026 (assuming no new tariffs), labor market weaker than data shows (job growth possibly negative), and non-tariff inflation near 2%; Fed cut rates defensively[1].

signals.raw: **Recent Fed projections**: Higher 2026 inflation expected, one rate cut penciled in for H2 2026 at earliest, citing tariffs (e.g., Trump's 15% blanket) and Middle East tensions[3].

signals.headline: **FOMC minutes (April 8, 2026)**: More officials see possible 2026 rate hikes due to oil/gas prices and inflation risks;
signals.raw: **FOMC minutes (April 8, 2026)**: More officials see possible 2026 rate hikes due to oil/gas prices and inflation risks; markets now expect no cuts until late 2027; March inflation forecast at 3.4% YoY[2].
